content.problemStatement:
Ada has two matrices AAA and BBB, each with NNN rows (numbered 111 through NNN) and NNN columns (numbered 111 through NNN).

Ada may perform the following operation any number of times (including zero): choose an integer iii (1‚â§i‚â§N1 \le i \le N1‚â§i‚â§N), then swap row iii and column iii in matrix AAA. (Formally, for each valid jjj, she swaps Ai,jA_{i, j}Ai,j‚Äã with Aj,iA_{j, i}Aj,i‚Äã.)

Is it possible to transform matrix AAA into matrix BBB?
constraints: 1‚â§Ai,j,Bi,j‚â§1091 \le A_{i, j}, B_{i, j} \le 10^91‚â§Ai,j‚Äã,Bi,j‚Äã‚â§109 for each valid i,ji, ji,j
the sum of N2N^2N2 over all test cases does not exceed 3‚ãÖ1063 \cdot 10^{6}3‚ãÖ106
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
NNN lines follow. For each iii (1‚â§i‚â§N1 \le i \le N1‚â§i‚â§N), the iii-th of these lines contains NNN space-separated integers Ai,1,Ai,2,‚Ä¶,Ai,NA_{i, 1}, A_{i, 2}, \ldots, A_{i, N}Ai,1‚Äã,Ai,2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,Ai,N‚Äã.
NNN more lines follow. For each iii (1‚â§i‚â§N1 \le i \le N1‚â§i‚â§N), the iii-th of these lines contains NNN space-separated integers Bi,1,Bi,2,‚Ä¶,Bi,NB_{i, 1}, B_{i, 2}, \ldots, B_{i, N}Bi,1‚Äã,Bi,2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,Bi,N‚Äã.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing the string `"Yes"` if it is possible to transform AAA into BBB or `"No"` if it is impossible (without quotes).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: We can swap the second row with the second column and in the resulting matrix, swap the first row with the first column.
